architecture: Runtime Governance, Operational Admissibility, Enterprise TrustMap, Manual Evidence Intake, Business Exposure Modeling, Role-Tailored Proof Pack, Dangerous Action Simulation, Executive Authority Layer, and planned Authenticity Trust Layer
core_thesis: AI is controlled before it acts, or it is not controlled at all.
core_question: Should this action be operationally admissible right now?
v51_extension_question: Can this person, product, vendor, website, ad, source, brand claim, payment request, or AI-generated artifact be trusted before someone acts on it?

v51_sources: Mike Rowe / Josh Smith transcript requirements supplied by Dr. Max Justice
v45_core_principle: CyberShield must not only measure trust. It must project trust.
v46_core_principle: Score confidence must explain itself through evidence used, evidence missing, reducers, improvers, owner, consequence avoided, and proof generated.
v51_core_principle: In the AI era, trust is not assumed. Trust is verified.
v51_positioning_line: When everything can be faked, CyberShield helps prove what can be trusted.
v46_implemented_modules: score authority cards with reducers and improvers
Runtime evidence used and evidence missing
Runtime consequence avoided and proof generated
Proof Pack confidence limits and score authority fields
ethical_boundary: Use psychology and trust design to improve clarity, trust perception, executive decision quality, customer protection, and evidence-backed action. Do not manipulate users, hide uncertainty, exaggerate capability, imply live enforcement where none exists, imply live takedown automation where none exists, obscure the prototype boundary, create artificial fear, or pressure users without evidence.
current_capabilities: Dangerous Action Simulation as the central executive story
Score authority cards with reducers and improvers
Runtime decision evidence: outcome, rationale, evidence used, evidence missing, owner required, consequence avoided, proof generated
Proof Pack includes score reducers, score improvers, evidence used, evidence missing, and confidence limits
transparent weighted trust model across ten operational trust factors
Decision ladder: Allow, Constrain, Escalate, Block
manual evidence library with accumulating artifacts
role-tailored Proof Pack with report recipient personalization
Architecture path from Advisory Prototype to Guided Pilot to Enterprise Trust Platform

business_exposure_model.status: directional planning estimate, not guaranteed financial calculation
business_exposure_model.purpose: create an executive business exposure moment and tie weak trust to operational cost risk

v51_planned_commercial_wedge: Brand Impersonation and Authenticity Risk Assessment

strategic_direction.ownership: MJC-owned advisory software, not standalone SaaS yet
strategic_direction.tooling_preference: low-cost and inspectable third-party tools when useful
strategic_direction.visual_style: CyberShield True Blue trust environment, not pale SaaS and not black command center

crm_direction.sync_status: admin-only payload and CSV/export path only; no live browser sync

critical_ux_rule: Every visible executive object must either explain itself on hover/focus, drill into detail, or move the user forward. No dead bubbles.
critical_business_rule: Executive users need a business exposure moment. CyberShield should translate weak trust, evidence gaps, unclear ownership, and eventually authenticity failures into a directional business exposure range. Treat this as a planning estimate, not a guaranteed financial calculation.
critical_architecture_rule: CyberShield must remain consolidated and performant. Do not reintroduce old phase-file module sprawl, broad MutationObserver loops, dynamic script injection, or timestamp-based runtime loading.
prototype_boundary: The current public build is a lightweight static prototype with local/export-oriented behavior only. It is not connected to live SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, GRC, CRM, cloud telemetry, Google Sheets sync, marketplace systems, ad platforms, takedown systems, or production agent enforcement systems.
commercial_entry_wedge: CyberShield Readiness Review or Operational Trust Assessment led by Maximum Justice Cybersecurity and Dr. Max Justice, vCISO, Security SME, Cybersecurity SME, U.S. veteran, and creator of The CHN vCISO GPT powered by Cyber Shield.
